design_philosophy.core_loop: Select country, compete in sequence of Olympic sporting events, accumulate performance scores across diverse disciplines (fencing, swimming, shooting, gymnastics, combat sports)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Execution precision under pressure—each event requires controller input mastery (button mashing, timing, directional control) to achieve qualifying scores
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Represent your nation in international Olympic competition; experience variety of athletic disciplines through skill-based gameplay
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses mechanical accessibility in favor of pure input-based challenge. No difficulty modes documented. Each event is a self-contained skill test rather than narrative-driven progression. Country selection adds lightweight world-building (29 nations) without story context.

difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resumption from last completed event, providing soft continue mechanic
difficulty.checkpointing: Event-by-event checkpoint via password; player must complete qualifying score in current event to progress
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: None documented—no difficulty settings or adjustable parameters. Skill floors are fixed per event. Zapper peripheral support for Gun Firing event offers alternate control scheme but does not lower difficulty

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential event progression in Olympic/Championship mode. Bonus events (Hang Gliding, Gun Firing) available between rounds as skill showcases. Training mode available for practice without progression impact
replayability_hooks: 29 playable countries with cosmetic differentiation (character portraits, representation)
Versus mode enables head-to-head competitive multiplayer across all events
Zapper gun peripheral support for Gun Firing event offers hardware variation
Password system encourages run completion and score-chasing across sessions

world_building_techniques: Country selection screen establishes national identity (Argentina, Japan, Soviet Union, etc.)
Cosmetic character portrait variations by region/nation (redrawn between Japanese and international versions to adjust aesthetics)
Olympic/Championship mode framing as international competition
Diverse event selection (fencing, taekwondo, canoeing) suggests global sport representation

faction_indicators: Regional censorship differences: Japanese version featured cigarettes/ashtray, international versions sanitized per Nintendo of America policies
danger_signals: Iron Curtain era nations included (Soviet Union, East Germany, Bulgaria, Poland) alongside Western democracies—cold-war geopolitical snapshot
environmental_storytelling: Seoul setting (Konami Sports in Seoul Japanese title) ties to 1988 Summer Olympics host city
Event variety (combat: taekwondo, fencing; aquatic: freestyle swimming, canoeing, high dive; precision: archery, clay pigeon shooting, horizontal bar) reflects Olympic Games curriculum
Character portrait aesthetics differ regionally, suggesting localization-driven world-building rather than canonical narrative

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor Track & Field II NES page)
